info.title: Geocodio
info.description:
Geocodio's RESTful API allows you to perform forward and reverse geocoding lookups. We support both batch requests as well as individual lookups.

You can also optionally ask for data appends such as timezone, Congressional districts or similar things of that nature.

servers.description: Geocodio API Endpoint for Enterprise Customers

tags.name: distance
tags.description: Calculate distances between one origin and one or more destinations

tags.name: distance-matrix
tags.description: Calculate distances between multiple origins and multiple destinations

tags.name: lists
tags.description: Upload and manage spreadsheets for batch geocoding

get.description: You can either use the `q` parameter for a full address query or a combination of `street`, `city`, `state`, `postal_code`, and/or `country` for when the address is already stored as separate fields on your end.

parameters.name: q
parameters.description: The query (i.e. address) to geocode, or a stable address key (e.g. gcod_usnbfvbm5l57cc8b8bnfnyrua9ym3) to resolve directly

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of fields to append

parameters.name: street
parameters.description: Can be used as an alternative to the `q` parameter

parameters.name: street2
parameters.description: Can be used as an alternative to the `q` parameter

parameters.name: city
parameters.description: Can be used as an alternative to the `q` parameter

parameters.name: county
parameters.description: Can be used as an alternative to the `q` parameter

parameters.name: state
parameters.description: Can be used as an alternative to the `q` parameter

parameters.name: postal_code
parameters.description: Can be used as an alternative to the `q` parameter

parameters.name: country
parameters.description: Defaults to USA

parameters.name: limit
parameters.description: Optional parameter. The maximum number of results to return. The default is no limit.

parameters.name: format
parameters.description: Optional parameter to change the output format of the JSON response to a different structure

parameters.name: destinations[]
parameters.description:
Array of destination locations for distance calculation. Each destination can be:
- A coordinate string: "lat,lng" or "lat,lng,id"
- A geocodable address string
When provided, each geocoded result will include a `destinations` array with distance/duration to each destination.

parameters.name: distance_mode
parameters.description:
Distance calculation mode. `straightline` calculates great-circle distance (as the crow flies) and does not return duration.
`driving` calculates driving distance and duration using road networks.

parameters.name: distance_units
parameters.description: Unit of measurement for distance filtering

parameters.name: distance_max_results
parameters.description: Maximum number of destinations to return per geocoded result

parameters.name: distance_max_distance
parameters.description: Maximum distance filter (in specified units)

parameters.name: distance_max_duration
parameters.description: Maximum duration filter in seconds (only applies to driving mode)

parameters.name: distance_min_distance
parameters.description: Minimum distance filter (in specified units)

parameters.name: distance_min_duration
parameters.description: Minimum duration filter in seconds (only applies to driving mode)

parameters.name: distance_order_by
parameters.description: Field to sort destinations by

parameters.name: distance_sort_order
parameters.description: Sort order for destinations

parameters.name: q

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of fields to append

parameters.name: destinations[]
parameters.description:
Array of destination locations for distance calculation. Each destination can be:
- A coordinate string: "lat,lng" or "lat,lng,id"
- A geocodable address string
When provided, each result will include a `destinations` array with distance/duration to each destination.

parameters.name: distance_mode
parameters.description: Distance calculation mode

parameters.name: distance_units
parameters.description: Unit of measurement for distance filtering

parameters.name: distance_max_results
parameters.description: Maximum number of destinations to return per result

parameters.name: distance_max_distance
parameters.description: Maximum distance filter (in specified units)

parameters.name: distance_max_duration

parameters.description: Field to sort destinations by

parameters.name: distance_sort_order
parameters.description: Sort order for destinations

parameters.name: skipGeocoding
parameters.description:
When set (empty value or `true`), skips the reverse geocoding pipeline and applies field appends directly to the supplied coordinates.
Set to `false` to explicitly disable. This is useful when you already have coordinates and only need field append data (e.g. timezone, census, congressional districts).
The `fields` parameter is required when using `skipGeocoding`. The response will only contain `location`, `accuracy_type` (set to `coordinate`), and `fields`.
No geocoding lookup is billed — only field appends are counted.

results.source: TIGER/Line® dataset from the US Census Bureau

get.summary: Calculate distance from one origin to multiple destinations
get.description:
Calculate distances and travel times from a single origin to one or more destinations.

Locations can be specified as:
- Coordinate strings: "lat,lng" or "lat,lng,id" (where id is an optional identifier)
- Geocodable address strings (will be geocoded automatically)

Results can be filtered and sorted using the optional filter parameters.

parameters.name: origin
parameters.description:
The origin location. Can be:
- A coordinate string: "lat,lng" or "lat,lng,id"
- A geocodable address string
- A stable address key (e.g. gcod_usnbfvbm5l57cc8b8bnfnyrua9ym3)

parameters.name: destinations[]
parameters.description:
Array of destination locations. Each can be:
- A coordinate string: "lat,lng" or "lat,lng,id"
- A geocodable address string
- A stable address key (e.g. gcod_usnbfvbm5l57cc8b8bnfnyrua9ym3)

parameters.name: mode
parameters.description:
Distance calculation mode:
- `driving`: Calculate driving distance and duration using road networks (default)
- `straightline`: Calculate great-circle distance (as the crow flies). Does not return duration.

parameters.name: units
parameters.description: Unit of measurement for distance filtering

parameters.name: max_results
parameters.description: Maximum number of destinations to return

parameters.name: max_distance
parameters.description: Maximum distance filter (in specified units)

parameters.name: max_duration
parameters.description: Maximum duration filter in seconds (only applies to driving mode)

parameters.name: min_distance
parameters.description: Minimum distance filter (in specified units)

parameters.name: min_duration
parameters.description: Minimum duration filter in seconds (only applies to driving mode)

parameters.name: order_by
parameters.description: Field to sort destinations by

parameters.name: sort_order
parameters.description: Sort order for destinations

X-BILLABLE-DISTANCE-CALCULATIONS.description: Number of distance calculations billed (may include multiplier for driving mode)

X-BILLABLE-LOOKUPS-COUNT.description: Number of geocode lookups performed (only present when addresses were geocoded)

value.error: The mode parameter must be one of: driving, straightline

403.description: Invalid API key, or other reason why access is forbidden.

post.summary: Calculate distance matrix (multiple origins to multiple destinations)
post.description:
Calculate distances and travel times from multiple origins to multiple destinations (distance matrix).

Locations can be specified as:
- Coordinate strings: "lat,lng" or "lat,lng,id"
- Coordinate objects: `{"lat": number, "lng": number, "id": string}`
- Geocodable address strings
- Stable address keys (e.g. gcod_usnbfvbm5l57cc8b8bnfnyrua9ym3)

The matrix size (origins × destinations) is limited to 10,000 by default.
Results can be filtered and sorted using the optional filter parameters.

X-BILLABLE-LOOKUPS-COUNT.description: Number of geocode lookups performed (only present when addresses were geocoded)

destinations: Matrix size (200 × 200 = 40000) exceeds maximum allowed size of 10000

origins.0: Each origin must be a valid coordinate (lat,lng or lat,lng,id), an object with lat/lng properties, or a geocodable address string

403.description: Invalid API key, or other reason why access is forbidden.

post.description:
Upload a CSV, TSV, or Excel file for batch geocoding. The file is processed asynchronously.

The file can be uploaded as a multipart form file, or the contents can be sent inline as a string with a separate `filename` parameter.

Use the `format` parameter to specify which columns contain address data using the template syntax `{{A}}`, `{{B}}`, etc.

parameters.name: fields
parameters.description: Comma-separated list of data append fields

file.description: The spreadsheet file to upload (CSV, TSV, Excel, or ZIP)

filename.description: Required if file contents are sent inline instead of as a file upload

direction.description: Whether to forward geocode (address to coordinates) or reverse geocode (coordinates to address)

format.description: Column format template, e.g. `{{A}} {{B}}, {{C}}` where A, B, C reference spreadsheet columns

callback.description: URL to receive a POST request when processing completes

headers.description: Column headers from the uploaded file

400.description: Invalid request (missing required fields, unreadable file, etc.)

403.description: Invalid API key, or feature not available.

Download the results of a completed spreadsheet list as a CSV file.

Only available when the list status is COMPLETED and the results have not expired (results are available for 72 hours after completion).

parameters.name: id

items.description: When using a JSON array, results are guaranteed to be returned in the same order as they are requested.

BatchGeocodeObject.description: If a JSON object is posted, you can specify a custom key for each element of your choice. This can be useful to match queries up with your existing data after the request is complete.

items.description: When using a JSON array, results are guaranteed to be returned in the same order as they are requested.

SkipGeocodingResult.description: Response when using the skipGeocoding parameter. Contains only location, accuracy_type, and fields — no address data.

fields.description: Appended field data based on the requested fields parameter

destinations.description: Distance calculations to destinations (only present when destinations parameter is provided)

place_fips.description: Census place FIPS code (returned for census2010 and earlier)

place.description: Census place information (returned for census2011 and later)

year.description: The redistribution year for the electoral boundaries

code.description: The federal electoral district unique identifier

is_upcoming_district.description: Whether this district is from an upcoming redistribution

FieldStatCan.description: Statistics Canada geographic data for Canadian addresses

cma_ca.description: Census Metropolitan Area or Census Agglomeration

Fields.description:
List of data fields to append to geocoding results.

ACS (American Community Survey) fields support optional geography suffixes to specify the geographic level:
`-block_group`, `-tract`, `-place`, `-msa`, `-county`, `-county_subdivision`, `-state`.
For example: `acs-demographics-tract` or `acs-economics-county`. If no suffix is provided, `block_group` is used by default.

DistanceMode.description:
The distance calculation mode:
- `driving`: Calculates driving distance and duration using road networks (default)
- `straightline`: Calculates great-circle distance (as the crow flies) using the Haversine formula. Does not return duration.

DistanceUnits.description: The unit of measurement for distance values

DestinationResult.description: A destination with calculated distance and duration

query.description: The original query string (coordinate or address)

id.description: Optional identifier if provided in the input (e.g., "lat,lng,id" format)

duration_seconds.description: Travel duration in seconds. Null for straightline mode.

geocode.description: Geocoding result if the destination was an address that was geocoded

OriginResult.description: An origin location with optional metadata

query.description: The original query string (coordinate or address)

geocode.description: Geocoding result if the origin was an address that was geocoded

origins_type.description: Type of origins input (list, coordinates, or addresses)

destinations_type.description: Type of destinations input (list, coordinates, or addresses)

total_calculations.description: Total number of distance calculations (origins × destinations)

calculations_completed.description: Number of calculations completed so far

download_url.description: URL to download results (only available when status is COMPLETED)

is_expired.description: Whether the job results have expired

name.description: A name for this distance matrix job

oneOf.description: List ID from a previously uploaded spreadsheet

max_results.description: Maximum number of destinations to return per origin

max_duration.description: Maximum duration filter in seconds (only applies to driving mode)

ListStatus.description: Status and details of an uploaded spreadsheet list

fields.description: Data append fields requested for this list

download_url.description: URL to download results (only available when status is COMPLETED and results have not expired)

expires_at.description: When the results will be deleted (72 hours after completion)

StableAddressKey.description: A unique, persistent identifier for this geocoding result. Stable address keys can be used in place of an address in any geocoding or distance request. Keys are returned for rooftop, point, range_interpolation, and street_center accuracy types. For unit-level results (API v1.11+), includes a dash-separated unit key suffix. Null for lower-accuracy results (place, county, state).

MatchType.description: Indicates the type of coordinate match. Possible values are "unit" (secondary address match, e.g. apartment or suite number), "building_centroid" (coordinates derived from building footprint centroid), "parcel_centroid" (coordinates derived from property parcel centroid), or null when no specific match type applies.
